what_it_does: An analysis note recording a 'family study' of five design-corpus source documents (vt-architects 4pp A4, vt-gatehouse 3pp, vt-property 2pp, vt-residential 2pp, vi-onepager 1pp 16:9). It stress-tests existing design-system hypotheses against these print/one-pager artifacts and records what each confirms about the surface axis, the LOD ladder, template parameterisation, and a couple of new atoms.
observations: Verdict line: confirms the A4-print surface and the tight (summary) end of the LOD ladder, and that client/audience is a TEMPLATE PARAMETER — 'No new grammar.'
Confirms stress-test items by id (G3 A4 print surface, C2 tight LOD end) — implying a tracked requirements register elsewhere.
Claims the surface axis now spans slide (16:9, 3:2) ↔ web (scroll) ↔ print (A4) with no grammar change.
Claims the LOD ladder spans summary(1pp) → terse-pitch(16pp) → standard(17pp) → high-detail(49pp).
★ marks 'strongest proof yet': vt-gatehouse/p-1 and vt-residential/p-1 are the same one-pager template filled with different client + prose → audience/client = a parameter, content = data.
★ identifies a new atom: a stage/progress chevron stepper whose accent slides along the gold→bronze ramp per variant (gatehouse gold, residential tan/bronze) → ramp used for variant theming.
Notes a comparison table reused across surfaces (vt-architects p-2 ↔ landing pricing table), section bands within a print page, an annotation callout/pointer atom, and a two-pane one-pager archetype.
Diff section uses ✅✅✅✅✅ / ✏️ / ➕ / ⚠️ markers and ends with '→ Docs updated' listing register changes (REQUIREMENTS G3+C2 → 🟢, AXES surface axis marked complete).
standouts: The discovery that the ramp (gold→bronze) is not just a fixed semantic role but a per-variant theming parameter (chevron recolor).
The 'same template, different client param' proof now confirmed across decks, web, AND print — claimed truly universal.
The whole note is a confirmation/no-contradiction study: it strengthens existing hypotheses rather than adding grammar.
conventions: Frontmatter-style header block (Sources / Status: ☑ analysed / Verdict) before the body.
★ to flag the highest-signal findings; ✅/✏️/➕/⚠️ symbols in the diff section.
References artifacts by id and page (vt-gatehouse/p-1, pitch-deck p-05) and requirement ids (G3, C2, D2).
Closes with a '→ Docs updated' provenance line naming which registry docs were changed.
notes: A corpus family-study that confirms the design system spans print/A4 and the summary LOD rung, and proves audience/client is a template parameter and the gold ramp can theme variants — no new grammar, two new atoms.
questions: Where are the source artifacts (vt-architects etc.) and the requirements register (G3/C2/D2) this note updates — what format are they in?
Were the page-count LOD figures (1/16/17/49pp) measured from the actual artifacts or estimated?
purpose_vs_actual: Match — it states itself as a family study confirming surface/LOD hypotheses and that is what it delivers, ending with the registry doc updates it triggered.
apparent_themes: Template + parameter (content as data, audience as a dial)
One grammar across surfaces (slide/web/print) and LODs (summary→high-detail)
Tokens/ramp as a parametric theming axis
Evidence-grounded confirmation against real artifacts (no fiction)
intention_signals: Trying to validate, against real client artifacts, that the design system's generative model (one containment grammar, axis dials, template+parameter) holds for print and the tight LOD end — and to harvest any reusable atoms the artifacts reveal (stepper, annotation callout, two-pane one-pager).
novelty: The empirical finding that the gold→bronze ramp position is itself a per-variant parameter (chevron recolors per client), and the cross-surface (print-included) confirmation of the template+audience-parameter mechanism.
contribution: Contributes the print/A4 surface and summary-LOD evidence plus the variant-theming-via-ramp insight and three reusable atoms (chevron stepper, annotation callout/pointer, two-pane one-pager archetype) into the system's grounded model.
summary_for_embedding: Design-corpus family study of five vt-* one-pagers/print artifacts confirming the A4-print surface and tight-LOD rung, proving audience/client is a template parameter and the gold ramp themes variants, adding stepper/annotation/two-pane atoms.
intention_for_embedding: To stress-test the design system's grammar against real print/one-pager client artifacts and confirm it spans all surfaces and LODs while harvesting any new reusable atoms.
